nodes.x: Lord, remember David, and all his afflictions:
And I will for this afflict the seed of David, but not for ever.
And David behaved himself wisely in all his ways; and the Lord was with him.
And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.
And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
How he sware unto the Lord, and vowed unto the mighty God of Jacob;
And Jacob said, Swear to me this day; and he sware unto him: and he sold his birthright unto Jacob.
And he said unto him, What is thy name? And he said, Jacob.
And the Lord said unto Jacob, Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred; and I will be with thee.
Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.
The Lord sent his word unto Jacob and it hath lighted upon Israel.
Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house, nor go up into my bed;
And I will set my tabernacle among you: and my soul shall not abhor you.
For I have not dwelt in an house since the day that I brought up Israel unto this day; but have gone from tent to tent, and from one tabernacle to another.
And I that am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.
Knowing that shortly I must put off this my tabernacle, even as our Lord Jesus Christ hath shewed me.
For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
I will not give sleep to mine eyes, or slumber to mine eyelids,
Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.
Consider and hear me, O Lord my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
Mine eyes prevent the night watches, that I might meditate in thy word.
Thou holdest mine eyes waking: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Let not them that are mine enemies wrongfully rejoice over me: neither let them wink with the eye that hate me without a cause.
Until I find out a place for the Lord, an habitation for the mighty God of Jacob.
Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Now will I bring again the captivity of Jacob, and have mercy upon the whole house of Israel, and will be jealous for my holy name;

this latter-day work, that it cannot be confounded with modern Christianity, for it has not the least proximity to the Gospel any more than a coat with many patches has to a coat without seam. “ The house of the God of Jacob ” is to be established in a place u sought out ” in the " top of the mountains,” where " a city of habitation” will be built for the “ redeemed of the Lord,” who will be…
And I will wait upon the Lord, that hideth his face from the house of Jacob, and I will look for him.
For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob, and will yet choose Israel, and set them in their own land; and the strangers shall be joined with them, and they shall cleave to the house of Jacob.
Hear ye, and testify in the house of Jacob, saith the Lord God, the God of hosts,
Lo, we heard of it at Ephratah: we found it in the fields of the wood.

¶ And the field of Ephron, which was in Machpelah, which was before Mamre, the field, and the cave which was therein, and all the trees that were in the field, that were in all the borders round about, were made sure

And the field of EphTon, which was in Mach* polnh, which was before Mamre, the field, and the cave which was therein, and all the tree* that were in the field, that were in all the borders round about, were made sore,
We will go into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstool.
Exalt ye the Lord our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.
And there I will meet with the children of Israel, and the tabernacle shall be sanctified by my glory.
And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the Lord, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, even a ram for a trespass offering.
And they shall keep his charge, and the charge of the whole congregation before the tabernacle of the congregation, to do the service of the tabernacle.
Arise, O Lord, into thy rest; thou, and the ark of thy strength.
Now therefore arise, O Lord God, into thy resting place, thou, and the ark of thy strength: let thy priests, O Lord God, be clothed with salvation, and let thy saints rejoice in goodness.
Go forth of the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
And thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which I shall give thee.
I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness; and let thy saints shout for joy.
And let these, thine anointed ones, be clothed with salvation, and thy saints shout aloud for joy. Amen, and Amen.
I will also clothe her priests with salvation: and her saints shall shout aloud for joy.
For thy servant David’s sake turn not away the face of thine anointed.
O Lord God, turn not away the face of thine anointed: remember the mercies of David thy servant.
And David said unto him, How wast thou not afraid to stretch forth thine hand to destroy the Lord’s anointed?
And David said unto him, Thy blood be upon thy head; for thy mouth hath testified against thee, saying, I have slain the Lord’s anointed.
I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him:
Who hast kept with thy servant David my father that thou promisedst him: thou spakest also with thy mouth, and hast fulfilled it with thine hand, as it is this day.
The Lord hath sworn in truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.
Then I will establish the throne of thy kingdom upon Israel for ever, as I promised to David thy father, saying, There shall not fail thee a man upon the throne of Israel.
Once have I sworn by my holiness that I will not lie unto David.
Then will I stablish the throne of thy kingdom, according as I have covenanted with David thy father, saying, There shall not fail thee a man to be ruler in Israel.
And what can David say more unto thee? for thou, Lord God, knowest thy servant.

Now therefore, O Lord God of Israel, keep with thy servant David my father that which thou hast promised him, saying, There shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit upon the throne of Israel; yet so that thy children take heed to their way to walk in my law, as thou hast walked before me.
If thy children will keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their children shall also sit upon thy throne for evermore.
¶ Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.

Moreover I will make a covenant of peace with them; it shall be an everlasting covenant with them: and I will place them, and multiply them, and will set my sanctuary in the midst of them for evermore.
And I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee in their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be a God unto thee, and to thy seed after thee.

And this shall be my covenant with you, ye shall have it for the land of your inheritance, and for the inheritance of your children forever, while the earth shall stand, and ye shall possess it again in eternity, no more to pass away.
For the Lord hath chosen Zion; he hath desired it for his habitation.
The Lord hath brought forth our righteousness: come, and let us declare in Zion the work of the Lord our God.
For the Lord hath chosen Jacob unto himself, and Israel for his peculiar treasure.
The Lord hath brought again Zion;The Lord hath redeemed his people, Israel,According to the election of grace,Which was brought to pass by the faithAnd covenant of their fathers.
This is my rest for ever: here will I dwell; for I have desired it.
For I lift up my hand to heaven, and say, I live for ever.
One thing have I desired of the Lord, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the Lord, and to inquire in his temple.

Brethren, I want to see you all with a desire above all other
times of my life. I feel as though I wanted to sit down with you
and seek the powers and gifts of God and the powers of Heaven, even
that which shall be shed forth for the restoration of the house of
Israel.
For behold, this is my work and my glory—to bring to pass the immortality and eternal life of man.
For this shall the earth mourn, and the heavens above be black: because I have spoken it, I have purposed it, and will not repent, neither will I turn back from it.
I will abundantly bless her provision: I will satisfy her poor with bread.
All her people sigh, they seek bread; they have given their pleasant things for meat to relieve the soul: see, O Lord, and consider; for I am become vile.
And I will bless her with blessings, and multiply a multiplicity of blessings upon her, and upon her generations forever and ever, saith the Lord your God. Amen.
¶ Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak comfortably unto her.

but in her temporal welfare she was not equally prosperous. She was destitute of bread and had nothing to sustain life. The minister kindly told her to exercise faith and she could make stones into bread. She thanked him for his timely advice, she had never thought of that, and accordingly went and procured some stones of suitable size for loaves of bread,
He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread: but he that followeth after vain persons shall have poverty enough.
I will also clothe her priests with salvation: and her saints shall shout aloud for joy.
Let thy priests be clothed with righteousness; and let thy saints shout for joy.
And I will kill her children with death; and all the churches shall know that I am he which searcheth the reins and hearts: and I will give unto every one of you according to your works.
For I will send into her pestilence, and blood into her streets; and the wounded shall be judged in the midst of her by the sword upon her on every side; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
For I, saith the Lord, will be unto her a wall of fire round about, and will be the glory in the midst of her.
There will I make the horn of David to bud: I have ordained a lamp for mine anointed.
I Jesus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things in the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, and the bright and morning star.
And behold, thou art David, and thou art called to assist; which thing if ye do, and are faithful, ye shall be blessed both spiritually and temporally, and great shall be your reward. Amen.
And hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;
His enemies will I clothe with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish.
For he must reign, till he hath put all enemies under his feet.
O my God, I trust in thee: let me not be ashamed, let not mine enemies triumph over me.

And out of his mouth goeth a sharp sword, that with it he should smite the nations: and he shall rule them with a rod of iron: and he treadeth the winepress of the fierceness and wrath of Almighty God.
He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.
